articles.title: BOF: Revenue doubled, capacity expanded, and major new orders set the stage for strong growth
articles.summary: BranchOut Food Inc. (BOF) has reported a significant increase in revenue, which more than doubled to $14.3 million, alongside an adjusted gross margin of 27.8%. The company is expanding its production capacity with a fourth line and has secured major new orders from Sam's Club and Target, signaling strong future growth. Annual revenue is projected to exceed $20 million, supported by diversified customer relationships.

articles.title: Ecommerce earnings recap: What you missed from Lovesac, Shoe Carnival and more
articles.summary: This article provides an ecommerce earnings recap for several retailers, including Lovesac and Shoe Carnival, highlighting their performance in the latest fiscal quarters. Lovesac experienced a 2.7% rise in net sales, with internet sales outpacing showroom sales, while Shoe Carnival saw a 3.4% decline in net sales, though its Shoe Station brand showed promising online growth. Other major retailers like Chewy, Alibaba, Amazon, Costco, Kroger, Home Depot, Target, and Walmart also reported their recent financial results, with many showing strong digital sales growth despite various market challenges.

articles.title: The Best 3 Retail Stocks to Buy and Hold for Decades
articles.summary: This article identifies three "Dividend Kings" in the retail sector that are suitable for long-term investment: Target (TGT), Lowe's (LOW), and Federal Realty (FRT). These companies have consistently increased their dividends for at least 50 consecutive years, demonstrating resilient business models. The authors suggest that despite current challenges, their proven ability to adapt makes them strong candidates for investors seeking reliable dividends and growth over decades.

articles.title: The Best 3 Retail Stocks to Buy and Hold for Decades
articles.summary: The retail sector contains risks due to fluctuating consumer preferences, but investors can mitigate this by choosing companies with a proven ability to adapt. This article highlights Target (TGT), Lowe's (LOW), and Federal Realty (FRT) as resilient "Dividend Kings" that have consistently increased dividends for 50+ consecutive years. These companies are presented as good long-term investments, despite current market challenges for some.

articles.title: Target Corp. stock rises Tuesday, still underperforms market
articles.summary: Shares of Target Corp. (TGT) increased by 2.04% to $121.20 on Tuesday, contributing to an overall positive trading session for the stock market. Despite this rise, Target Corp. still underperformed the broader market, which saw the S&P 500 Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average climb significantly. The company's stock closed 3.81% below its 52-week high of $126.00.
